metadata.description: A platform namespace — the isolation and scheduling boundary within a cluster that workloads (VMs, containers, pods) are placed into. Models the construct that Kubernetes calls a Namespace, OpenShift calls a Project, and other platforms express as a tenant-scoped partition of cluster resources. Provider-specific because namespace semantics vary across platforms, but the role — tenant-scoped resource boundary within a cluster — is universal. A provider declares required_inputs that reference this type so placement and enrichment policies can select the right namespace for a request.

context.purpose: Declares the isolation boundary inside a cluster that workloads are placed into and tenancy binds to.
context.plain_description: What Kubernetes calls a Namespace and OpenShift calls a Project: a named partition of a cluster that workloads live in. It binds a tenant, carries labels and annotations, and cannot outlive its cluster. Providers and placement policies use it to answer which namespace a request lands in; the realized name and platform UID are published for provider-side references to resolve.
use_when: You need workloads partitioned per tenant, team, or environment inside a shared cluster.
You need quota and placement policy to operate on a governed namespace record, not an ad-hoc string.
not_for: The consumption limits inside the boundary — Platform.ResourceQuota constrains a namespace; it doesn't define one.
works_with: Compute.Cluster — the cluster the namespace exists within.
Platform.ResourceQuota — hard limits scoped to this namespace.
Compute.Container / Software.Service — workloads placed into it.

adopts.role: cluster-scoped isolation boundary for workloads and resource quotas

lifecycle.decommission: Provider deletes the namespace and all resources within it. Decommission is cascading — all contained resources must be decommissioned first or decommissioned as part of the namespace teardown.

name.description: The namespace name within the cluster (e.g. 'tenant-alpha-prod'). Unique within the containing cluster.

cluster_ref.description: Resource reference to a Compute.Cluster (common-elements §2.5, ADR-025). Authored by handle; deferred resolution supported (claim-before-define).

oneOf.description: Short form — the handle of an existing Compute.Cluster.

tenant_uuid.description: The tenant this namespace is bound to. Resources placed into this namespace inherit this tenant binding.

name.description: The realized namespace name — the string value provider-specific namespace references resolve to (Provider-Class `SharedDataElement` references, ADR-038).

uid.description: The platform-native UID of the namespace (e.g., Kubernetes metadata.uid).

relationships.name: cluster
relationships.description: The cluster this namespace exists within. A namespace cannot outlive its cluster.
